title: Grant Telegram sessions full Prometheus/workspace access instead of scoped workspace mount
summary: Trace and fix the runtime/session context used for Telegram chat handling so Telegram sessions can access the full Prometheus/workspace directory, matching the intended behavior noted in telegram-channel.ts. Also update misleading comments if needed and verify file tools resolve against the full workspace root.
details:
## Problem
`src/gateway/telegram-channel.ts` claims Telegram has 'Full workspace file access in personality context' (line 9), but the observed behavior shows Telegram sessions only see a narrow mounted workspace plus separate `src/` access. The Telegram channel file itself does not configure workspace roots; it only forwards messages into `handleChat(...)`.

## Root cause hypothesis
The filesystem restriction is likely imposed in the runtime/tool-context construction that sits behind the injected `handleChat` dependency, not inside `telegram-channel.ts`. Telegram is probably being created with a session/workspace root that points at a scoped subdirectory rather than the full `Prometheus/workspace` root.

## Proposed implementation
1. Trace the call path where `TelegramChannel` is instantiated and where its injected `handleChat` dependency resolves tool access/session roots.
2. Identify the code that determines workspace root / allowed filesystem paths for Telegram-originated sessions.
3. Change that code so Telegram direct interactive sessions use the full `Prometheus/workspace` directory as their workspace root.
4. Preserve any existing security restrictions for other channels/sandboxed contexts unless explicitly intended otherwise.
5. Update the misleading comment in `src/gateway/telegram-channel.ts` if necessary so comments match actual behavior.
6. Verify by checking that file listing tools from Telegram can see the expected full workspace contents, not just the currently scoped subset.

## Acceptance criteria
- Telegram interactive sessions can access all files under `Prometheus/workspace` using normal workspace file tools.
- `src/` read/edit proposal behavior remains unchanged unless intentionally expanded.
- No regression for non-Telegram channels.
- Comments/documentation accurately reflect the final behavior.

## Notes
The only source file currently visible in `src/` from this session is `gateway/telegram-channel.ts`, so the exact implementation files still need to be traced during execution. The executor should inspect the runtime bootstrap / dependency injection path that creates `TelegramChannel` and `handleChat`.

affectedFiles.description: Update misleading comment if behavior changes or clarify that access is determined by injected runtime context
Adjust Telegram session workspace root / filesystem scope to full Prometheus/workspace in the code that builds tool context

estimatedImpact: Restores expected Telegram file access, removes confusing workspace scoping behavior, and aligns actual runtime behavior with operator expectations.
executorPrompt: Investigate why Telegram direct interactive sessions cannot access the full Prometheus/workspace directory. Start by tracing where src/gateway/telegram-channel.ts is instantiated and where its injected handleChat dependency builds runtime/tool/file access context. Identify the exact code that scopes filesystem access for Telegram sessions. Then implement a fix so Telegram direct sessions use the full Prometheus/workspace directory as their workspace root while preserving existing restrictions for other channels unless necessary. Update misleading comments in src/gateway/telegram-channel.ts if needed. Run any relevant verification/build steps if available, and report the exact files changed, the root cause, and how the fix was verified.
